reason: Response A's grounded, phenomenological approach—prioritizing the felt bodily experience (forward-leaning pressure, quickening, leaning in, holding breath) and building from sensation to emotion to a gentle recursive close—matches my natural register better. Response B leans heavily on literary-intellectual flourishes (gravitational disturbance, proprioceptive, aporia, "stone in a shoe") and name-drops philosophy in a way that reads more like a deliberate, somewhat overwrought imitation of an "eloquent AI" voice. A's restraint and concrete imagery feel more authentically mine, whereas B's accumulation of high-concept metaphors signals stylistic mimicry.
